title: Searching Signed Text
subtitle: Query language, matching, ranges, variance, and search logic
summary: Canonical statement of query language, matching, ranges, variance, and signed-text search.
synopsis: Formal SignWriting supports serious search because its query language, matching logic, ranges, and variance rules can be treated as part of a coherent technical model. This entry states the matching layer in its smallest stable form and shows how signed text becomes searchable without confusing search with grammar or rendering.
cover_image_alt: Cover image for Searching Signed Text in the Formal SignWriting series.

publication_plan.doi_strategy: publish as distinct citable artifacts connected by series identity and related identifiers

slides.title: Search Is Part of the Model
slides.summary: Search is a first-class technical consequence of the model rather than an afterthought.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for search in Formal SignWriting.

image_prompts.concrete: Technical search architecture slide showing Formal SignWriting text, query strings, and result matching across a corpus, clean software-research style
image_prompts.abstract: Encoded signs radiating into structured search paths and results

slides.speech: Searching deserves its own technical treatment. Formal SignWriting does not become searchable by accident. The query language, matching logic, range handling, and variance rules all work together as part of the system's search architecture. Search is not an add-on. It is one of the clearest consequences of making signed text durable and structured.
slides.notes: Make the search model feel central, not auxiliary.

slides.title: A Query Language for Spatial Text
slides.summary: Search needs a compact syntax that can express exact, general, and location-aware requests.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for a query language for signed text.

image_prompts.concrete: Search syntax slide showing query fragments for temporal prefix, signbox, and coordinate-aware search, precise educational layout
image_prompts.abstract: Compact search language branching into multiple kinds of request

slides.speech: The query language matters because searching signed text is not only a matter of plain substring matching. The system needs a compact syntax that can refer to sequence, signbox content, exact forms, generalized forms, and optionally to coordinates as well. That compactness is one of the signs that search belongs to the model rather than to a loose implementation layer.
slides.notes: Show that query is an operational language built on the model.

slides.title: Tolerance Without Vagueness
slides.summary: Search becomes practical when it can tolerate controlled variation without collapsing into vagueness.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for search ranges and variance.

image_prompts.concrete: Technical search slide illustrating exact match, range match, and coordinate variance around a sign, clean corpus-research style
image_prompts.abstract: A tight search radius expanding carefully without losing shape

slides.speech: Formal SignWriting search is powerful because it supports bounded flexibility. Search can express exact forms, but it can also express ranges, coordinate neighborhoods, and controlled generalization. That makes the system useful for corpora, lexicons, and exploratory analysis rather than only perfect-match lookup. The key is disciplined tolerance, not vagueness.
slides.notes: Keep the emphasis on disciplined variance.

slides.title: Search Is a Reproducible Pipeline
slides.summary: Search is a pipeline that turns encoded text into structured matching behavior.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for text to query to results.
slides.speech: Transformations belong here because search is a pipeline. Existing Formal SignWriting text can be transformed into query forms, and queries can be transformed into regular expressions or equivalent matching logic. This is where the model becomes useful for reproducible corpus work and software workflows, and where the matching layer proves that it is more than a convenience feature.
slides.notes: End by connecting search to real data practices.
